records.ety: [L. haereticus, Gr. <?/ able to choose, heretical, fr. <?/ to take, choose: cf. F. hrtique. See Heresy.]
usage: A heretic is one whose errors are doctrinal, and usually of a malignant character, tending to subvert the true faith. A schismatic is one who creates a schism, or division in the church, on points of faith, discipline, practice, etc., usually for the sake of personal aggrandizement. A sectarian is one who originates or is an ardent adherent and advocate of a sect, or distinct organization, which separates from the main body of believers.

defs: One who holds to a heresy; one who believes some doctrine contrary to the established faith or prevailing religion.
quotes: A man that is an heretic, after the first and second admonition, reject.

defs: One who having made a profession of Christian belief, deliberately and pertinaciously refuses to believe one or more of the articles of faith “determined by the authority of the universal church.”
